ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus 'Operation Dream Job' DLL side-loading via trojanized signed application
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Lazarus / Diamond Sleet / Labyrinth Chollima delivery chain: a benign-but-signed binary (trojanized PuTTY, Sumatra PDF, TightVNC, AmberLogic, Notepad++ plugin, fake job-description PDF reader, etc.) is dropped to a user-writable path and side-loads a malicious sibling DLL (e.g. PEBBLEDASH, MagicRAT, BLINDINGCAN, ImparseRAT, LightlessCan) when launched. Distinct from generic side-loading because we require the LOADER to be a known-good vendor binary executing OUTSIDE its normal install path — the exact 'job offer' pattern reported by Mandiant, Microsoft (Diamond Sleet), and
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_hash) as parent_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("putty.exe","sumatrapdf.exe","tightvnc.exe","vncviewer.exe","notepad++.exe","7zfm.exe","foxitreader.exe","pdfreader.exe","acrord32.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_path NOT IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","*\\Windows\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.parent_process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest parent_process_id [| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | rename Filesystem.process_id as parent_process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where like(file_path, parent_process_path."%")] | rename file_name as sideloaded_dll file_hash as sideloaded_sha256 | table firstTime dest user parent_process_name parent_process_path sideloaded_dll sideloaded_sha256
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus / Diamond Sleet — trojanized signed binary side-loading
let _LazarusLoaders = dynamic([
    "putty.exe","puttygen.exe","plink.exe",
    "sumatrapdf.exe",
    "tightvnc.exe","vncviewer.exe",
    "notepad++.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe",
    "foxitreader.exe","acrord32.exe",
    "vmwarehostopen.exe","hxtsr.exe",
    "flashupdate.exe","officeupdater.exe"
]);
let _UserWritablePaths = dynamic([@"\Users\",@"\AppData\",@"\Downloads\",@"\Temp\",@"\Public\",@"\ProgramData\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_LazarusLoaders)
// Loader is running from a user-writable path — NOT its normal install dir
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_UserWritablePaths)
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)")
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
// Sideloaded DLL is co-located with the loader (classic side-load layout)
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
// Lazarus payload DLLs are typically unsigned/no version info
| where isempty(InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName)
   or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName !has_any ("Microsoft","Adobe","Simon Tatham","Foxit","TightVNC","VMware","Don Ho","Igor Pavlov")
   or DeviceId in ((DeviceImageLoadEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
        | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_LazarusLoaders)
        | where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
        | where FileName endswith ".dll"
        | summarize KnownDlls = make_set(FileName) by DeviceId
        | mv-expand KnownDlls
        | distinct DeviceId))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          LoaderProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideLoadedDll = FileName,
          SideLoadedSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Lazarus / Diamond Sleet — trojanized signed binary side-loading (ASIM)
let _LazarusLoaders = dynamic([
    "putty.exe","puttygen.exe","plink.exe","sumatrapdf.exe",
    "tightvnc.exe","vncviewer.exe","notepad++.exe","7zfm.exe",
    "foxitreader.exe","acrord32.exe","vmwarehostopen.exe",
    "flashupdate.exe","officeupdater.exe"]);
let _UserWritablePaths = dynamic([@"\Users\",@"\AppData\",@"\Downloads\",@"\Temp\",@"\Public\",@"\ProgramData\"]);
let _LoaderRuns = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where TargetProcessName has_any (_LazarusLoaders)
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (_UserWritablePaths)
          or TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (_UserWritablePaths)
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, TargetProcessName,
              TargetProcessId, TargetProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (_UserWritablePaths)
| join kind=inner _LoaderRuns on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (TimeGenerated1 - 5m .. TimeGenerated1 + 5m)
// Sibling DLL co-located with the loader's working directory
| extend LoaderDir = tostring(split(ActingProcessName,"\\")[-2])
| where TargetFilePath has LoaderDir
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          Loader = TargetProcessName1,
          LoaderCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine1,
          SideLoadedDll = TargetFileName,
          SideLoadedPath = TargetFilePath,
          SideLoadedSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus Operation Dream Job - Signed App DLL Side-Loading from User Path
id: 8c7c8fa9-1c4f-4a8e-9d77-3a2c0c9e1d11
status: experimental
description: Detects the Lazarus / Diamond Sleet / Labyrinth Chollima 'Dream Job' chain in which a legitimate signed app (PuTTY, SumatraPDF, TightVNC, Notepad++, Foxit) executes from a user-writable path and side-loads a sibling DLL.
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/10/18/multiple-north-korean-threat-actors-exploiting-the-teamcity-cve-2023-42793-vulnerability/
    - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/lightless-can-lazarus-dream-job
    - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2023/09/29/lazarus-luring-employees-trojanized-coding-challenges-case-spanish-aerospace-company/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/03/15
tags:
    - attack.execution
    - attack.persistence
    - attack.defense_evasion
    - attack.t1574.002
    - attack.t1036.005
    - attack.g0032
logsource:
    category: image_load
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_loader:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\putty.exe'
            - '\sumatrapdf.exe'
            - '\tightvnc.exe'
            - '\vncviewer.exe'
            - '\notepad++.exe'
            - '\foxitreader.exe'
            - '\7zfm.exe'
    selection_userpath:
        Image|contains:
            - '\Users\'
            - '\AppData\'
            - '\Downloads\'
            - '\Temp\'
            - '\Public\'
            - '\ProgramData\'
    selection_dll:
        ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
    filter_legit_install:
        Image|contains:
            - '\Program Files\'
            - '\Program Files (x86)\'
    filter_signed:
        Signed: 'true'
        SignatureStatus: 'Valid'
    condition: selection_loader and selection_userpath and selection_dll and not filter_legit_install and not filter_signed
falsepositives:
    - Portable apps run from a USB stick by power users
    - Custom IT tooling that intentionally ships portable copies of these apps
level: high
ucs.rationale: Lazarus Group has used this exact loader-from-user-path → sibling-DLL pattern across nearly every major campaign since 2019: Operation Dream Job (LinkedIn job lures dropping trojanized PDF readers and PuTTY-derived plink loaders side-loading PEBBLEDASH/MagicRAT), the 2022 ASMI engineer/Spanish aerospace intrusions documented by ESET (trojanized SumatraPDF + ImparseRAT side-load), the 3CX downstrea

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus FudModule BYOVD: vulnerable signed driver dropped + service-installed to disable EDR
ucs.description: Hunts the BYOVD (Bring-Your-Own-Vulnerable-Driver) chain attributed by Microsoft and AhnLab to Diamond Sleet / Lazarus FudModule rootkit campaigns: drop a known-vulnerable signed kernel driver (ene.sys, dbutil_2_3.sys, ASRock asrdrv101.sys, viragt64.sys, etc.) to a user-writable path then register it as a service so the rootkit can read/write kernel memory and blind EDR. This isn't a generic 'driver dropped' rule — it scopes to the specific drivers Lazarus is repeatedly observed weaponising and correlates the file drop with the SCM service registration that turns it into a kernel load.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as drop_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("created","renamed") AND Filesystem.file_name="*.sys" AND (Filesystem.file_name IN ("ene.sys","dbutil_2_3.sys","viragt64.sys","rentdrv2.sys","appsvc32.sys","fkbpfk.sys","wnbios.sys","asrdrv101.sys","nicm.sys","rwdrv.sys","iqvw64e.sys","physmem.sys","gdrv.sys","mhyprot2.sys") OR (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Users\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Public\\*") AND NOT Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\*"))) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as svc_time values(Registry.registry_value_data) as image_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*\\ImagePath" AND Registry.registry_value_data="*.sys" by Registry.dest Registry.registry_path Registry.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`] | where svc_time>=drop_time AND svc_time<=(drop_time+1800) AND like(image_path, "%".file_name."%") | table drop_time svc_time dest user file_name file_path file_hash registry_path image_path
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus / Diamond Sleet FudModule BYOVD — vulnerable signed driver drop + service install
let _LazarusBYOVDDrivers = dynamic([
    "ene.sys",          // ENE Tech — primary FudModule loader
    "dbutil_2_3.sys",   // Dell DBUtil — CVE-2021-21551
    "viragt64.sys",     // Vir.IT eXplorer
    "rentdrv2.sys",
    "appsvc32.sys",
    "fkbpfk.sys",
    "wnbios.sys",
    "asrdrv101.sys",    // ASRock — used in Aug-2023 Lazarus campaign
    "nicm.sys",
    "rwdrv.sys",
    "iqvw64e.sys",      // Intel
    "physmem.sys",
    "gdrv.sys",         // Gigabyte
    "mhyprot2.sys"      // miHoYo / Genshin Impact anti-cheat
]);
let _UserWritable = dynamic([@"\Users\",@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Public\",@"\Temp\",@"\Windows\Temp\"]);
let driverDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".sys"
    | where FileName in~ (_LazarusBYOVDDrivers)
         or (FolderPath has_any (_UserWritable)
             and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\drivers\")
             and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\"))
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DriverName = FileName, DriverPath = FolderPath, DriverSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropperProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              Account = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let svcInstalls = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData endswith ".sys"
    | extend SvcDriverFile = tolower(tostring(split(RegistryValueData,"\\")[-1]))
    | project SvcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, ServiceKey = RegistryKey,
              ImagePath = RegistryValueData, SvcDriverFile,
              SvcInitiator = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SvcInitiatorCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
driverDrops
| join kind=inner svcInstalls on DeviceId
| where SvcTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| where SvcDriverFile == tolower(DriverName)
| project DropTime, SvcTime, DeviceName, Account,
          DriverName, DriverPath, DriverSHA256,
          ServiceKey, ImagePath,
          DropperProcess, DropperCmd, SvcInitiator, SvcInitiatorCmd
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Lazarus FudModule BYOVD — vulnerable signed driver drop + SCM service registration
let _LazarusBYOVDDrivers = dynamic([
    "ene.sys","dbutil_2_3.sys","viragt64.sys","rentdrv2.sys",
    "appsvc32.sys","fkbpfk.sys","wnbios.sys","asrdrv101.sys",
    "nicm.sys","rwdrv.sys","iqvw64e.sys","physmem.sys",
    "gdrv.sys","mhyprot2.sys"]);
let _UserWritable = dynamic([@"\Users\",@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Public\",@"\Temp\"]);
let driverDrops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".sys"
    | where TargetFileName in~ (_LazarusBYOVDDrivers)
         or (TargetFilePath has_any (_UserWritable)
             and not(TargetFilePath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\drivers\")
             and not(TargetFilePath startswith @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\"))
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              DriverName = tolower(TargetFileName),
              DriverPath = TargetFilePath, DriverSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
              DropperProcess = ActingProcessName, ActorUsername;
let svcInstalls = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 7045   // service installed
    | extend ImagePath = tostring(EventData)
    | parse EventData with * 'ImagePath">' ImagePathFromXml '<' *
    | extend SvcDriverFile = tolower(tostring(split(ImagePathFromXml,"\\")[-1]))
    | where SvcDriverFile endswith ".sys"
    | project SvcTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname = Computer,
              ServiceName, ImagePathFromXml, SvcDriverFile,
              SvcInitiator = SubjectUserName;
driverDrops
| join kind=inner svcInstalls on DvcHostname
| where SvcTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| where SvcDriverFile == DriverName
| project DropTime, SvcTime, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DriverName, DriverPath, DriverSHA256,
          ServiceName, ImagePathFromXml, DropperProcess, SvcInitiator
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (Diamond Sleet, Aug 2023), AhnLab (March 2024 FudModule rootkit report), and Avast (Feb 2024 admin-to-kernel report on FudModule v2) all document Lazarus weaponising specific vulnerable signed drivers — most notably ene.sys, asrdrv101.sys, dbutil_2_3.sys and mhyprot2.sys — to disable Defender, Sentinel and other PPL-protected EDR processes before deploying the RustBucket / LightlessCan /

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ImagePathFromXml` is not a canonical column of ImFileEvent, SecurityEvent.
